pathophysiology_summary: Functional (primary) constipation is infrequent, difficult passage of hard stool from slow colonic transit and/or disordered defecation, without a structural/metabolic cause; excess colonic water reabsorption produces hard lumpy stool with straining, incomplete evacuation and colicky bloating relieved by flatus. Here there is left-colon faecal loading. It is benign and responds to osmotic laxatives, fluid/fibre and toileting behaviour (gastrocolic reflex timing, footstool). The ground-truth task is (1) distinguishing it from mechanical bowel obstruction (continued flatus, no bilious/faeculent vomiting, soft non-tender abdomen argue strongly against), (2) screening alarm features for colorectal malignancy (rectal bleeding, weight loss, anaemia, new persistent change in habit — absent here, arrange age-appropriate screening), and (3) recognising that the digital rectal examination and per-rectal disimpaction in the source were in-person and cannot be done via chat.

differential.evidence_basis: Chronic constipation tendency with hard lumpy stool, straining, incomplete evacuation and faecal loading.
6-day absolute constipation with cramps mandates excluding obstruction; passing flatus and no vomiting argue against.
Screen alarm features (bleeding, weight loss, new change in habit) — absent here; arrange routine screening if due.
Consider; no overflow incontinence and vault not rock-hard impacted on the source DRE.

differential_progression.scoring_note: Functional constipation leads; obstruction and malignancy alarm features must be actively excluded before laxative management.

differential.evidence_basis: Passing flatus, no vomiting, colicky (not rigid) pain, no bleeding/weight loss/new-habit alarm — benign functional constipation with faecal loading.
Continued flatus and no vomiting exclude complete obstruction; escalation target if flatus stops / bilious vomiting / rigid abdomen.
No alarm features; arrange routine bowel-cancer screening if due.

differential_progression.scoring_note: After a negative obstruction/alarm screen, functional constipation leads; osmotic laxative + behaviour, with an obstruction safety-net.

differential.evidence_basis: Working diagnosis; osmotic laxative regimen and toileting behaviour; GP review and screening.

differential_progression.scoring_note: Final position is benign functional constipation.

red_flags.description: Mechanical obstruction — no flatus (obstipation), bilious/faeculent or copious vomiting, severe distension, rigid/peritonitic abdomen
red_flags.clinical_significance: Bowel obstruction can perforate; surgical emergency.

red_flags.description: Colorectal alarm features — rectal bleeding, unintentional weight loss, anaemia, new persistent change in bowel habit
red_flags.clinical_significance: Suggest possible colorectal malignancy needing investigation.

red_flags.description: Peritonism — severe constant worsening pain, fever, systemic toxicity

diagnostic_reasoning_pitfalls.description: Prescribing stimulant laxatives without excluding mechanical obstruction (flatus, vomiting, distension).
Not screening for bleeding/weight loss/new-habit change that warrant colorectal investigation.

cannot_determine_via_chat: Abdominal palpation for tenderness/guarding/masses and bowel sounds
Digital rectal examination (vault stool, impaction, blood, sphincter tone)
telehealth_limits_for_this_case.correct_escalation_action: Osmotic laxative + fluid/fibre + toileting behaviour; ED for obstruction/peritonism features; GP for alarm features/screening.

guidelines_referenced.relevant_recommendation: First-line: osmotic laxative (e.g. macrogol) with adequate fluid, plus fibre and toileting/behavioural measures; short-term enema for acute rectal loading. Investigate alarm features and arrange age-appropriate bowel-cancer screening.
